metadata.seed: Yeah, I guess not. But all my other teammates won their matches. It made me feel dumb to be the only one who lost. Like I'm a terrible wrestler. I don't feel like I belong on the team anymore.

patient.query: Yeah, I guess not. But all my other teammates won their matches. It made me feel dumb to be the only one who lost. Like I'm a terrible wrestler. I don't feel like I belong on the team anymore.

automatic_thoughts: I feel dumb for losing
I'm a terrible wrestler
I don't belong on the team

llm_response.response: I can see how you might be feeling that way, but it's important to remember that everyone has their own unique strengths and weaknesses. Losing a match doesn't define your worth as a wrestler or a team member. Let's talk about what we can do to help you feel more confident in your abilities.

alternative_perspective.response: "I can understand how you might be feeling discouraged after seeing your teammates win, but it's important to remember that everyone has their own journey. What do you think a trusted friend or family member would say about your performance?"

safety_check.response: I'm really sorry to hear that you're feeling this way, but I want you to know that it's okay to have bad days. It can be tough when things don't go as planned, but remember that everyone has their own struggles. Let's talk about what might be helping you feel better or any thoughts you have about why this happened.

patient.query: I get what you're saying, but it's hard to shake off that feeling after losing. I know it shouldn't feel like my whole worth is tied to one match, but it just… does. I guess I get really overwhelmed thinking about my skills, and sometimes it just feels like I'm never good enough, no matter how hard I try.

I'm never good enough
I get overwhelmed thinking about my skills

patient.query: Yeah, I know in my head that it's just one match, but it... it still feels huge, you know? I mean, I put so much into it, and then when it doesn’t go well, it feels like all my effort just disappears. I guess I just want to feel like I matter, like my hard work is recognized.

automatic_thoughts: all my effort just disappears
I want to feel like I matter
my hard work is not recognized

patient.query: Thanks, I appreciate that. It’s just… sometimes I feel like no matter what I do, I’m still invisible. Like, I put in the hours and the dedication, but then it feels like nobody sees it or acknowledges it. I guess it makes me doubt if I'm even good at what I love.

automatic_thoughts: nobody sees my efforts
I doubt my abilities

llm_response.response: I understand how you feel, it can be disheartening when our efforts don't seem to be recognized. Let's explore together why you might be feeling this way and if there are any patterns we can identify.

safety_check.response: I can see how that might feel really tough, and it's understandable to doubt yourself when you don't feel seen. Let's talk more about what you mean by 'invisible.'

I guess I've always been the one trying to prove myself, to show that I can do things well. So when it doesn’t land, it’s like I’m back at square one, questioning everything.

patient.query: Yeah, I mean, I guess I can trace it back to a few moments. Like, there was this time in class where I shared an idea, and the teacher shot it down in front of everyone. I just felt so small and embarrassed. Since then, I’ve been really hesitant to share. Those memories just replay in my mind, and it makes it hard to take risks or put myself out there again.

I am embarrassed
I can't take risks
I shouldn't put myself out there
